introduction: As we aim to redesign our loyalty program, it's crucial to balance the preferences of both our older and younger customer demographics. This presentation explores key considerations for this process.

subsections.heading: Understanding Gen Z/Millennial Digital Preferences
subsections.content:
• Current customer demographics reveal Gen Z and Millennials constitute 62% of Lee's Market's active customer base as of Q4 2025.
• This trend signifies a shift towards a younger, digitally inclined customer base, necessitating a digital-first loyalty scheme.
• Gen X and Boomer shoppers, though lower in proportions (38%), are a revenue-stable segment, requiring consideration in the loyalty program design.
• A balanced approach is essential to cater to both the tech-savvy Gen Z/Millennial customer base and traditional Gen X/Boomer customers.
• The loyalty program redesign should incorporate digital strategies that resonate with younger customers, including mobile apps and social media initiatives.
• Simultaneously, traditional engagement methods should be maintained to satisfy Gen X/Boomer customers' preferences.
• Continuous monitoring and evaluation of customer preferences across all generations will ensure our loyalty program remains flexible and adaptable.
subsections.justification: This content emphasizes the importance of balancing tech innovations for Gen Z/Millennials and traditional methods for Gen X/Boomers in redesigning the loyalty program.

subsections.heading: Meeting Gen X/Boomer Traditional Service Expectations
subsections.content:
• Gen X/Boomers appreciate physical store experiences and human customer service
• They value quality over quantity and are less likely to be influenced by discounts
• This demographic tends to be brand loyal and appreciates personalized service
• Direct mail and email are preferred modes of communication
• Gen X/Boomers still appreciate traditional payment methods like cash and credit cards
• They value the convenience of home delivery and in-store pick-up options
subsections.justification: These points describe Gen X/Boomer service expectations but don't address specific considerations for loyalty program redesign.

subsections.heading: Balancing Traditional and Digital in Loyalty Program
subsections.content:
• Offering both digital and physical loyalty cards can satisfy diverse customer preferences
• Incorporating personalized offers in both online and offline channels can enhance customer experience
• Ensuring seamless integration between online and in-store shopping can improve customer convenience
• Providing options for digital and traditional payments can cater to different demographic preferences
• Maintaining a balance between instant gratification and long-term rewards can keep customers engaged
subsections.justification: These points describe balancing traditional and digital aspects in a loyalty program, but don't directly answer the DR Question's specific considerations.

conclusion: By understanding and integrating the preferences of both our younger and older customer base, we can create a loyalty program that appeals to all. The next step involves refining our program based on these insights and piloting it in select stores.
